definitions.en: to bring or carry something to a place
definitions.vi: mang hoặc chuyển một cái gì đó đến một nơi
definitions.es: llevar o cargar algo a un lugar

example.vi: Bạn có thể đem sách cho tôi không?
example.en: Can you bring the book to me?

exampleSentences: Anh có thể đem tập tài liệu này đến phòng họp giúp em không?
Chị nhớ đem áo mưa khi ra ngoài vì trời sắp mưa.
Em sẽ đem bánh ngọt đến buổi tiệc sinh nhật của bạn.
notes_md:
**đem** = to bring (carry something to another place or person). It focuses on moving something away from where you are now to somewhere else.

It’s a verb, used with physical objects most of the time: đồ, tiền, quà, giấy tờ, thức ăn. Very close to **mang** (also “bring/carry”) — in many cases they’re interchangeable, but *đem* often feels a bit more deliberate or purposeful.


It can also mean “take” depending on perspective, but the core idea is still moving something somewhere.

content.sentence: Bạn có muốn ___ đồ ăn không?

translation.en: Do you want to bring food?

content.sentence: Tôi sẽ ___ món quà cho bạn.

translation.en: I will bring a gift for you.

notes:
đem = to bring (carry something to another place or person). It focuses on moving something away from where you are now to somewhere else.

It’s a verb, used with physical objects most of the time: đồ, tiền, quà, giấy tờ, thức ăn. Very close to mang (also “bring/carry”) — in many cases they’re interchangeable, but đem often feels a bit more deliberate or purposeful.

It can also mean “take” depending on perspective, but the core idea is still moving something somewhere.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Can you bring these documents to the meeting room for me?
Remember to bring a raincoat when going outside because it is about to rain.
I will bring cakes to my friend's birthday party.
